page: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, configured with 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core to deliver 4 vCPUs. The hardware allocation includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with a 236 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2156.0 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 31.35 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth performance show some limitations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is relatively weak at 3991.37, and the uncached memory bandwidth read-only large block test registered at 36.24 GB/sec. Database operations, measured via Passmark, achieved an average score of 2871.72, while Redis SET operations performed at a weaker tier of 5.66 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E4ds_v6 provides a dense memory profile and local storage without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute. The presence of advanced CPU instruction sets, such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, supports specialized tasks like low-latency vector operations and small-scale LLM inference, where it achieves 149.21 tokens/sec for text generation on a small 135M model. This server type is best suited for relational databases, caching layers, text processing, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs from a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB of memory per core, and includes a 236 GB local disk with Premium Storage capabilities. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance demonstrates strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2156.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 31.35 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth are more modest. With advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512, the server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand extensive multi-core parallelism. It is well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, text processing, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 236 GB local disk, delivering strong single-core performance for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v6 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 236 GB local disk, ideal for databases and text processing.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated high-frequency Intel Xeon processing, substantial RAM-to-core density, and integrated local storage.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz with 4 vCPUs
32.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio
236 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core performance in text processing and gzip compression
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for specialized vector workloads
Optimized for relational databases, caching layers, and memory-bound applications
